questions.question: What is a Circular Linked List?

options.A: A type of doubly-linked list where the last node points back to the first node
options.B: A type of singly-linked list with no head node
options.C: A type of doubly-linked list with only forward links
options.D: A type of singly-linked list with a fixed size

questions.explanation: A Circular Linked List is a type of data structure where the last node points back to the first node, forming a circular loop.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of a Circular Linked List?

options.A: It allows fast insertion and deletion of nodes at any position
options.B: It conserves memory by not allocating space for the head node
options.C: It simplifies the implementation of traversal and searching algorithms
options.D: It improves the performance of sorting and merging operations

questions.explanation: The main advantage of a Circular Linked List is that it simplifies the implementation of traversal and searching algorithms, as the last node always points to the first node.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of traversing a Circular Linked List?

options.A: O(n), where n is the number of nodes
options.B: O(log n), where n is the number of nodes
options.C: O(1), since the last node always points to the first node
options.D: O(n^2), where n is the number of nodes

questions.explanation: The time complexity of traversing a Circular Linked List is O(n), where n is the number of nodes, since we need to visit each node once.

questions.question: What is the space complexity of a Circular Linked List?

options.A: O(n), where n is the number of nodes
options.B: O(log n), where n is the number of nodes
options.C: O(1), since we only need to store the last node
options.D: O(n^2), where n is the number of nodes

questions.explanation: The space complexity of a Circular Linked List is O(n), where n is the number of nodes, since we need to store each node and its link to the next node.

questions.question: How do you implement the remove operation in a Circular Linked List?

options.A: By simply deleting the node at the specified position and updating the links of the adjacent nodes
options.B: By first traversing the list to find the node to remove and then deleting it
options.C: By keeping track of the current node and removing it after traversal
options.D: By using a doubly-linked list implementation

questions.explanation: To implement the remove operation in a Circular Linked List, you delete the node at the specified position and update the links of the adjacent nodes.

questions.question: How do you detect a cycle in a Circular Linked List?

options.A: By using Floyd's Cycle Detection algorithm, which uses two pointers that move at different speeds
options.B: By simply traversing the list to find the node where the cycle begins
options.C: By keeping track of the visited nodes to detect the cycle
options.D: By using a doubly-linked list implementation

questions.explanation: You can detect a cycle in a Circular Linked List using Floyd's Cycle Detection algorithm, which uses two pointers that move at different speeds.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of detecting a cycle in a Circular Linked List?

options.A: O(n), where n is the number of nodes
options.B: O(n^2), where n is the number of nodes
options.C: O(1), since we only need to traverse the list once
options.D: O(log n), where n is the number of nodes

questions.explanation: The time complexity of detecting a cycle in a Circular Linked List is O(n), where n is the number of nodes, since we need to traverse the list once to detect the cycle.

questions.question: How do you implement the merge operation in a Circular Linked List?

options.A: By merging the two lists into a new list and updating the links of the adjacent nodes
options.B: By simply concatenating the two lists
options.C: By keeping track of the current node and merging the lists after traversal
options.D: By using a doubly-linked list implementation

questions.explanation: To implement the merge operation in a Circular Linked List, you merge the two lists into a new list and update the links of the adjacent nodes.

questions.question: What is the space complexity of the merge operation in a Circular Linked List?

options.A: O(n + m), where n and m are the number of nodes in the two lists
options.B: O(max(n, m)), where n and m are the number of nodes in the two lists
options.C: O(min(n, m)), where n and m are the number of nodes in the two lists
options.D: O(n^2), where n is the number of nodes

questions.explanation: The space complexity of the merge operation in a Circular Linked List is O(n + m), where n and m are the number of nodes in the two lists, since we need to create a new list to store the merged nodes.

questions.question: How do you implement the insert operation at the beginning of a Circular Linked List?

options.A: By creating a new node and updating the links of the adjacent nodes
options.B: By simply traversing the list to find the node before the insertion point and updating the links
options.C: By keeping track of the current node and inserting the new node after traversal
options.D: By using a doubly-linked list implementation

questions.explanation: To implement the insert operation at the beginning of a Circular Linked List, you create a new node and update the links of the adjacent nodes.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of inserting a node at the beginning of a Circular Linked List?

options.A: O(1), since we only need to update the links of the adjacent nodes
options.B: O(n), where n is the number of nodes
options.C: O(log n), where n is the number of nodes
options.D: O(n^2), where n is the number of nodes

questions.explanation: The time complexity of inserting a node at the beginning of a Circular Linked List is O(1), since we only need to update the links of the adjacent nodes.

questions.question: How do you implement the delete operation for a node at the beginning of a Circular Linked List?

options.A: By updating the last node to point to the new first node
options.B: By simply removing the node at the beginning and updating the links of the adjacent nodes
options.C: By keeping track of the current node and deleting the node after traversal
options.D: By using a doubly-linked list implementation

questions.explanation: To implement the delete operation for a node at the beginning of a Circular Linked List, you update the last node to point to the new first node.

options.A: O(1), since we only need to update the last node
options.B: O(n), where n is the number of nodes
options.C: O(log n), where n is the number of nodes
options.D: O(n^2), where n is the number of nodes
